Thoughts on my own death depend greatly on the manner and time of death I envision. If I envision death coming because of the mass deceiving of many people, in five years' time or less, I would feel a shivering existential fear. If I die in a high-speed accident, I will feel brief fear mixed with a great equinamity, an equiamity of the innocent, I guess. If I die of natural causes at old age (at least half a century after this year), I believe I would feel a great sense of peace, and no great hurry to prolong life, especially with the great friend group I envision I will have up to my final days. I envision dying in bed, of course, surrounded by my children.
Peace, sorrow, silence
